rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR CHURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/27/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel with the following issues: 1) The corrected I&A referred to in the fee base calculation as having been filed "April 2025" has not been filed. 2) Tolls and mileage are not reimbursable. LR 14.57. 3) Petitioner requests $1,660 for "payment for expenses charged for annual client record maintenance and administration". Please specify what this cost is and provide the invoices. 4) Attorney requests xo fees for services prior to appointment but xo fees should be for services provided to petitioner while she was acting as personal representative. If no further declaration is filed, fees will be reduced by $765. 5) The amended petition (at Par. 32) indicates that Victor Iglesias' address is 1010 S. Atlantic Blvd, Apt #20, Alhambra, CA; however, the 7/3/25 filing shows that he was given notice at 1010 S. Atlantic Blvd, without a designation of apartment number. The notice filed on 7/15/25 shows that he was given notice at 6333 Palm Ave, Bell Gardens, CA. Please clarify what his address is.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SHARON CARNEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/8/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The beginning value of assets on hand do not agree with the I&As on file. It appears that the burial trust was inventoried/appraised two times, but a Corrected I&A was not filed. (2) What happened to the gold earrings and gold crown signet ring (J013 and J016)? (3) When did Conservator hire Diana Tate-Andretich to appraise the tangible personal property? She has photographs, so she presumably entered the apartment sometime before May 30/2024, when the probate referee verified his appraisal. Why was she not contacted to reappraise the assets, given Ed Mueller's findings? Did he also submit photographs of the valueless items? (4) What is meant by automatic asset rebalancing (Polaris account)? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by9/24/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY B. PORTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/6/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service has been filed. (3) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) Items 5a(2) and 5b of the petition must be answered. (5) Item 10 of the petition is incorrect, as the holographic does name co-executors. (6) Proof of Holographic Instrument must be filed. (7) Please file a copy of the vesting deed. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/22/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Attachments 7a and 7b should each be for a one-half community property interest, not just the legal description for the entire property.)

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 8/3/2011. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status hearing will be set for 2/2/27. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before that status hearing date, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF VICTOR POLANCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared or addressed at the hearing: (1) Incomplete attestation to verify declaration [BMD-002] as it is signed but not dated. (2) The court may request additional information regarding the absence of an official record. (3) Any documents submitted for court records must be properly translated in English. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
